items.title: Permafrost: Freeze Claude Code's prompt prefix to cut DeepSeek bills by 64%
items.why_it_matters: If you are using Claude Code to drive cheaper models like DeepSeek, prompt caching is key to keeping costs low. Permafrost freezes the prompt prefix in Claude Code, ensuring your conversation history hits the cache consistently and reducing your API bill by up to 64%. You can install this tool today to optimize your Claude Code terminal setup and run your development loops for pennies.
items.action: Clone the repository and run it alongside your Claude Code session today to immediately lower your DeepSeek API costs.

items.title: macOS menu bar gauges for your Claude Code quota
items.why_it_matters: As a heavy user of Claude Code, hitting your daily rate limits mid-build can completely ruin your momentum. This lightweight open-source macOS utility puts your remaining Claude Code token quota directly in your menu bar so you always know how much runway you have left. You can install it on your Mac in five minutes to keep an eye on your usage while building AI-ingest.
items.action: Download the app from the repo and run it on your Mac today to monitor your Claude Code limits at a glance.

items.title: /dmg: A persistent memory and session sync skill for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: When vibe coding, restarting your terminal session usually means losing the context of what you and the AI were working on. This tool adds a custom skill to Claude Code that persists memory and syncs your session state across restarts. You can add this to your setup this week to stop wasting time re-explaining your Next.js project structure to Claude every time you open a new terminal.
items.action: Install the skill into your local Claude Code configuration this week to keep your project context alive across sessions.

items.title: Papermill Press: An AI-friendly markup language for PDF generation
items.why_it_matters: Generating PDFs in Next.js usually requires heavy libraries or headless Chrome hacks that break easily. Press is an XML-based document language designed specifically for LLMs to generate clean, multi-page PDFs in a single shot without manual pagination. If you want to add a 'download summary' or 'export report' button to your Next.js projects, this makes it incredibly easy for Claude to write the templates.
items.action: Sign up for their free API tier and ask Claude to write a Press template to generate a clean PDF summary of your AI-ingest feed this week.
